clinical_history: Very elderly woman with ECG showing which ONE of following?
diagnosis: ECG shows sinus rhythm (you can march out sinus P waves at rate of about 85/min) with complete AV heart block and a ventricular paced rhythm (VVI) at about 70/min. None of P waves is conducted. Whenever you see an LBBB pattern, always look for hidden pacemaker spikes, which may be of low amplitude. P wave prominent and morphology speak to left atrial abnormality. ST-T changes, discordant with QRS, are secondary to asynchronous pacemaker activation of the ventricles, causing the wide QRS. This patient would be candidate for developing "memory T waves" if the pacing were intermittent.
